forensic_flags.narrative: Days-payable of ~100 days (FY2025) is far above the ~30-60 day norm for most industries — a hallmark of supply-chain finance / reverse factoring, where supplier payment terms are extended through a bank facility but the balance is booked as trade payables, not debt. This is effectively a callable bank facility that can vanish overnight, so it belongs in economic debt even though the face of the balance sheet shows none of it.
forensic_flags.pointer: Read the accounts-payable footnote and any 'supplier finance program' / 'supply-chain finance' disclosure (ASU 2022-04, ASC 405-50), plus MD&A liquidity.
